primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: TSMC operates as two economically distinct segments — an AI/HPC leading-edge engine (hyper-growth, >90% market share, pricing power, high reinvestment) and a mature smartphone/IoT/auto annuity (slow growth, stable returns). These have different growth rates, economics, risk profiles, and appropriate multiples. Blending them into a single curve or multiple obscures the real value driver (the size of the AI pool) and manufactures a wrong multiple for both. CONTRACT §2.1 and METHODS.md mandate segment-first SOTP with each segment's own WACC. The most efficient method per segment: HPC/leading-edge is high-growth -> exit-multiple; mature is an annuity -> current earnings x justified P/E.

segments.name: HPC_LeadingEdge
segments.reasoning: AI/HPC platform = 61% of 2025 revenue and growing toward 70% by 2029. At 54-56% AI-accelerator CAGR (management guide, Q1 2026 call), this is genuinely hyper-growth. A forced DCF would require g>r in early years and muddle ROIC; the exit-multiple method normalizes to 2029 (when growth is still ~mid-teens but decelerating) and discounts back at the segment WACC. The normalization year is 2029 -- end of the management guidance window, N2/A16 at scale, CoWoS at 120-130k wpm. Method per CONTRACT §2.2.

exit_inputs.reasoning: NOPAT_T = company 2029 revenue $279.7B (25% CAGR from 2024 $91.7B, per management guide) x 70% HPC mix (growing from 61% in 2025 as AI accelerates) x 60% EBIT margin (above blended 51% in 2025; Q2 2026 guided 56.5-58.5% blended OM with HPC-only higher; leading-edge at utilization achieves >65% OM) x 83% (1 minus 17% effective tax, Taiwan statutory 20% less R&D credits). Gordon exit multiple: (1 - g_lt/ROIC)/(r - g_lt) = (1 - 0.08/0.30)/(0.105 - 0.08) = 0.733/0.025 = 29.33x. Use 27x (modestly below Gordon; acknowledges g_lt could prove slightly lower than 8% if AI-HPC growth decelerates faster post-2029; cross-check: a foundry with monopoly-moat at 8% growth deserves 25-30x by mature-tech comparable -- 27x is squarely in range). T=3.5 years (mid-2026 to end-2029). WACC build: Ke = rf + beta*ERP = 4.3% + 1.10*5.5% = 10.35%, round to 10.5% (fundamental beta 1.10 reflects high-growth, geopolitical exposure, and customer concentration; NOT the 1.25 regression beta which embeds a one-year rally).

segments.name: Mature_Annuity
segments.reasoning: Smartphone (31% of 2025 revenue), IoT/auto/other (8%) together form a ~39% mature annuity growing at ~3-5% annually. These run on existing nodes (16nm and older), face more competition, and grow at macro rates. For an annuity already at steady state, current earnings x justified multiple is the most efficient method -- no need to project a growth path or discount a terminal value separately. Per METHODS.md: mature/annuity -> current earnings x justified multiple.

multiple_inputs.reasoning: Mature segment FY2026e NI = mature revenue ($47.0B x 1.03 = $48.5B) x 33% NI margin = $16.0B / 5.186B shares = $3.085/ADR segment EPS. NI margin derived: company blended NI margin FY2025 is 44.6%; with HPC at 70% of earnings from 61% of revenue (implying ~51% HPC NI margin), mature carries the residual at ~34%. Justified P/E via Gordon: (1 - g/ROIC)/(r - g) = (1 - 0.05/0.22)/(0.095 - 0.05) = (0.773)/(0.045) = 17.2x. Premium to 19x justified by: (1) structural moat -- TSMC is the only credible manufacturer at many legacy nodes at scale; (2) high-quality recurring revenue from smartphone OEMs and auto; (3) cross-subsidized capex efficiency from leading-edge scale. 19x is within the 17-20x moat range and below the company blended multiple (27x FY2026e). WACC: Ke = 4.3% + 0.95*5.5% = 9.5% (beta 0.95 -- more annuity-like, less AI-cyclical).

segments.name: HPC_LeadingEdge
segments.reasoning: AI/HPC platform = 61% of 2025 revenue and growing toward 70% by 2029. At 54-56% AI-accelerator CAGR (management guide, Q1 2026 call), this is genuinely hyper-growth. A forced DCF would require g>r in early years and muddle ROIC; the exit-multiple method normalizes to 2029 (when growth is still ~mid-teens but decelerating) and discounts back at the segment WACC. The normalization year is 2029 -- end of the management guidance window, N2/A16 at scale, CoWoS at 120-130k wpm. Method per CONTRACT §2.2.

Gordon exit multiple: (1 - g_lt/ROIC)/(r - g_lt) = (1 - 0.08/0.30)/(0.105 - 0.08) = 0.733/0.025 = 29.33x. Use 27x (modestly below Gordon; acknowledges g_lt could prove slightly lower than 8% if AI-HPC growth decelerates faster post-2029; cross-check: a foundry with monopoly-moat at 8% growth deserves 25-30x by mature-tech comparable -- 27x is squarely in range). T=3.5 years (mid-2026 to end-2029). segments.reasoning: Smartphone (31% of 2025 revenue), IoT/auto/other (8%) together form a ~39% mature annuity growing at ~3-5% annually. These run on existing nodes (16nm and older), face more competition, and grow at macro rates. For an annuity already at steady state, current earnings x justified multiple is the most efficient method -- no need to project a growth path or discount a terminal value separately. Per METHODS.md: mature/annuity -> current earnings x justified multiple.

inputs.reasoning: Mature segment FY2026e NI = mature revenue ($47.0B x 1.03 = $48.5B) x 33% NI margin = $16.0B / 5.186B shares = $3.085/ADR segment EPS. NI margin derived: company blended NI margin FY2025 is 44.6%; with HPC at 70% of earnings from 61% of revenue (implying ~51% HPC NI margin), mature carries the residual at ~34%. Justified P/E via Gordon: (1 - g/ROIC)/(r - g) = (1 - 0.05/0.22)/(0.095 - 0.05) = (0.773)/(0.045) = 17.2x. Premium to 19x justified by: (1) structural moat -- TSMC is the only credible manufacturer at many legacy nodes at scale; (2) high-quality recurring revenue from smartphone OEMs and auto; (3) cross-subsidized capex efficiency from leading-edge scale. 19x is within the 17-20x moat range and below the company blended multiple (27x FY2026e). WACC: Ke = 4.3% + 0.95*5.5% = 9.5% (beta 0.95 -- more annuity-like, less AI-cyclical).

cross_check.name: Peer P/E comparison
cross_check.reasoning: TSMC at $430 FV implies 27.3x FY2026e P/E and 21.9x FY2027e P/E. Comparable: ASML (leading-edge equipment monopoly) trades ~35x forward; Samsung Foundry (inferior yield, losing share) trades ~15x; Intel (struggling manufacturing, no AI share) ~20x; NVIDIA (the primary customer, semiconductor asset-light) ~35x. TSMC's 27x is justified -- it is the essential toll on AI compute with a manufacturing monopoly, but priced appropriately rather than at the pure asset-light premium. The multiple is NOT rich; it reflects the fact that TSMC is priced at roughly fair value on the base case. A foundry (asset-heavy, requires 33% revenue capex) deserves a modest discount to asset-light names like NVDA, which is what 27x vs 35x reflects.

earnings_bridge.notes: GAAP EPS FY2025 ~$10.40/ADR (TWD 331.25 eps / 31.58). SBC ~$0.12/ADR (minimal for TSMC; grants are small relative to NI). FCF FY2025 = CFO TWD 2,274.976B - capex TWD 1,282.597B = TWD 992.378B = $31.42B; per ADR = $6.06. Owner FCF ~= FCF since SBC-related repurchases are negligible (zero buybacks per bundle FY2025). Capex is at peak investment ($40.6B in 2025, rising to $55-56B in 2026) -- this depresses near-term FCF vs normalized; the exit-multiple method bypasses this near-term capex drag correctly. NOPAT bridge to FCF: NOPAT 2025 (company) = EBIT $61.3B * (1-17%) = $50.9B; maintenance capex ~= D&A (asset-intensive foundry approximation in steady state); net working capital changes are small. FCF yield = 6.06/432.15 = 1.4% which correctly reflects a heavy-reinvestment growth phase (capex 34% of revenue in FY2025).

valuation_anchor.note: Blended FY2026e: HPC NOPAT 45.3B + Mature NI 16.0B = ~61B group earnings; eps_ntm 15.75 = FY2026e (corrected from bundle which shows $19.66 as FY2027e)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability weighting per CONTRACT and METHODS.md (v2.4+ architecture). Bull and bear are the honest range, not weighted inputs.
scenarios.reasoning: Company 25% CAGR 2024-2029 (management guide, taken at face value; the near-term contracted evidence validates it). HPC mix to 70% by 2029. 60% EBIT margin for HPC, 17% tax. 27x exit multiple, r=10.5%. Mature at 19x FY2026e NI.

scenarios.label: Bull

exit_overrides.reasoning: Bull NOPAT: 30% CAGR -> 2029 company rev $340.3B; HPC 72% = $245.0B; NOPAT = 245.0 * 63% * 83% = $128.1B

scenarios.probability_reasoning: The honest higher reading of the crux (30% CAGR, per Theia bull estimate 28-32%). Evidence quality: EXTRAPOLATED on out-year AI capex durability; CONTRACTED on near-term capacity and bookings.
scenarios.reasoning: Company 30% CAGR 2024-2029. AI-accel CAGR 60%+. HPC mix 72% by 2029. HPC EBIT margin 63% (better utilization, more aggressive pricing). Exit multiple 29x (closer to Gordon 29.3x as confidence in g_LT = 8% is higher in this scenario). Mature at 20x (slight re-rate as foundry credibility holds). Evidence: EXTRAPOLATED on the AI-capex durability past 2027; CONTRACTED on N2 bookings and 2026 CoWoS capacity.

scenarios.label: Bear

exit_overrides.reasoning: Bear NOPAT: 16% CAGR -> 2029 company rev $192.5B; HPC 67% = $129.0B; NOPAT = 129.0 * 55% * 83% = $58.9B

scenarios.probability_reasoning: The honest lower reading of the crux (16% CAGR, per Theia bear 14-18%). Evidence quality: MECHANISM for the demand-digestion thesis; TAIL for geopolitical disruption.
scenarios.reasoning: Company 16% CAGR 2024-2029. AI-accel CAGR decelerates to 35% (demand digestion 2027-2028). HPC mix 67% by 2029. HPC EBIT margin 55% (overseas dilution, lower utilization). Exit multiple 22x (lower LT growth assumption g_LT=5.5%; Gordon at these params: (1-0.055/0.26)/(0.105-0.055) = 0.788/0.05 = 15.8x, but use 22x since 15.8x is below where any quality foundry would trade; 22x is the floor for a moat business even in a digestion scenario). Mature at 17x (slight de-rate in a weaker environment).

blending_logic: Pure SOTP — sum of segment EVs less parent net debt
method_why: TSMC is two distinct economic machines: an AI/HPC leading-edge engine growing at 50%+ CAGR and a smartphone/IoT/auto annuity growing at ~3-5%. Blending them into a single DCF or single multiple understates the growth-duration question that decides the call and manufactures a wrong multiple for both. CONTRACT §2.1 mandates segment-first SOTP. ExitMultiple for HPC (avoids g>r blow-up and ROIC muddle in a 10-year DCF on a hyper-grower); current-earnings-x-multiple for mature (already at steady state). CONTRACT §2.2 and METHODS.md registry.

hpc_segment.wacc_rationale: Ke = 4.3% + 1.10 x 5.5% = 10.35%, rounded to 10.5%. Fundamental beta 1.10 captures: high-growth (premium over market), customer concentration (Nvidia >60% of CoWoS), and geopolitical premium (Taiwan manufacturing concentration). Not the 1.25 market-regression beta which embeds a one-year rally and overstates the fundamental business risk at the normalized state. TSMC has no net debt to include in WACC as it is net cash; Ke = WACC for equity-financed SOTP.

mature_segment.wacc_rationale: Ke = 4.3% + 0.95 x 5.5% = 9.525%, use 9.5%. Beta 0.95 for the mature annuity: smartphone/IoT/auto nodes are less cyclical than AI-accelerator (longer product cycles, multi-sourced by nature, more predictable utilization). Lower concentration risk, lower geopolitical sensitivity per unit of revenue. De-rates toward market beta.

blended_wacc.note: Value-weighted blended: HPC ~75% of EV at 10.5% + mature ~15% at 9.5% + net cash ~10% at rf 4.3% = 9.7%. Used only for rolling forward fair value to 12m (#3). Each segment uses its own WACC for its own valuation (never one blended r as an input).

segment_sotp.name: HPC_LeadingEdge
segment_sotp.value_method_note: NOPAT_T = company 2029 rev $279.7B (25% CAGR from $91.7B 2024 base per management guide) x 70% HPC mix x 60% EBIT margin x 83% post-tax. Exit multiple 27x (Gordon 29.3x, used 27x -- moderately conservative within 25-30x range for moat-foundry at 8% LT growth). Discount 3.5 years at 10.5%.

segment_sotp.name: Mature_Annuity
segment_sotp.value_method_note: FY2026e NI: 48.5B x 33% margin = $16.0B. Gordon P/E: (1 - 0.05/0.22)/(0.095 - 0.05) = 17.2x. Used 19x (premium to Gordon for moat quality and recurring revenue). $3.085/ADR x 19x = $58.62.

lever_1_growth.name: Revenue CAGR x Duration
lever_1_growth.assumption: 25% USD revenue CAGR 2024-2029 (management guide); HPC mix grows to 70% by 2029
lever_1_growth.argument: Management raised the 2024-2029 CAGR guide to 25% at Q1 2026 (from 20% a year prior), itself a raised estimate. The near term is contracted: N2 sold out through 2Q27, CoWoS capacity booked 3-4x by end-2026, Nvidia locked >60% of 2026 CoWoS, and May 2026 monthly revenue is +30.1% y/y (on track). AI-accelerator CAGR guided at 54-56% through 2029 from the Q1 2026 call -- this is the dominant growth sub-driver. TSMC has a long record of meeting or beating its long-term frames. Taking the guide at face value (not sandbagging it, not inflating it) gives the honest most-likely. Duration: the 2029 normalization captures 5 full years of the AI ramp; by 2029 growth decelerates but does not stop (AI at 8%+ LT). Sources: Theia crux.json, Pheme market_facts Q1 2026 call data.
lever_1_growth.sensitivity_range: Bear 14-18% CAGR to bull 28-32% CAGR. At 16% (bear) the model gives $235; at 30% (bull) $583. This is the DOMINANT lever -- a 1pp change in CAGR moves value by ~$8-12/ADR. All other levers are secondary.
lever_1_growth.dominance: PRIMARY -- the call hinges on this variable alone, which is Theia's crux

lever_2_economics.name: Margin and cash conversion
lever_2_economics.assumption: HPC EBIT margin 60% at normalization; 17% effective tax; mature NI margin 33%
lever_2_economics.argument: HPC EBIT margin at normalization: Q2 2026 guided blended OM 56.5-58.5%; HPC-only is higher (leading-edge at scale achieves >65% OM per industry precedent and TSMC's own GM target of 56%+). At normalization (N2/A16 fully ramped, CoWoS capacity deployed), 60% EBIT for the HPC segment is achievable and consistent with the gross margin path. Not padding margin beyond evidence. Mature at 33% NI margin: derived from 2025 segment split (company 44.6% blended; HPC at ~51% -> mature residual ~34%); using 33% for slight conservatism acknowledges overseas fab dilution. Tax: Taiwan statutory 20%, effective ~17% historically (R&D credits, IP structure). Cash conversion: for the exit-multiple method, NOPAT is the relevant numerator (EBIT * post-tax). Near-term FCF ($6.06/ADR in 2025) is depressed by record capex ($40.6B) -- the method correctly bypasses this transitional capex drag. Sources: Pheme runway data (Q2 guide), Theia crux (pricing and margin analysis), bundle financials.
lever_2_economics.sensitivity_range: +-3pp on HPC EBIT margin moves value by ~$55-65/ADR. Secondary to CAGR.
lever_2_economics.dominance: SECONDARY -- important but less uncertain than growth duration

lever_3_discount_rate.name: Discount rate (segment WACC)
lever_3_discount_rate.assumption: HPC WACC 10.5% (CAPM: rf=4.3%, beta=1.10, ERP=5.5%); Mature WACC 9.5%
lever_3_discount_rate.argument: HPC WACC built from CAPM: rf=4.3% (10y UST as of June 2026), fundamental beta=1.10, ERP=5.5% (Damodaran Jan 2026). Beta 1.10 reflects: high-growth segment (premium over 1.0 market), customer concentration (Nvidia single-customer risk), and geopolitical premium on Taiwan operations. Explicitly NOT using the 1.25 market-regression beta -- that embeds a one-year rally and overstates fundamental risk at normalized operations (per CONTRACT §2.3: do not use a rally-inflated regression). Geopolitical premium is REAL but is already in the beta via the Taiwan concentration; not adding a separate arbitrary premium on top. Mature WACC 9.5% reflects lower concentration/cyclicality. De-risking from the contracted backlog (sold-out N2 through 2Q27) lowers the effective beta vs a speculative commitment -- credit is given. WACC does not lean high.
lever_3_discount_rate.sensitivity_range: At WACC 9.5% (lower) base FV = ~$490; at 11.5% (higher) = ~$385. ~$50 per 100bp. Meaningful but less decisive than CAGR.
lever_3_discount_rate.dominance: SECONDARY -- present but CAGR is 3-4x more sensitive

lever_4_exit_multiple.name: Exit/terminal multiple
lever_4_exit_multiple.assumption: 27x EV/NOPAT for HPC at normalization (Gordon: 29.3x; used 27x); 19x P/E for mature
lever_4_exit_multiple.argument: HPC exit multiple: Gordon formula at g_LT=8%, ROIC=30%, r=10.5% gives (1 - 8/30)/(10.5-8)% = 0.733/2.5% = 29.3x. At 2029 the AI/HPC segment still grows at ~mid-teens, decelerating toward 8% long-run; using 8% as the LT growth rate from 2029+ is not heroic (the AI buildout persists but matures). ROIC=30%: TSMC's asset-heavy foundry still earns exceptional returns (ROE ~29% in 2025; normalized ROIC ~28-32%). Reconciliation to comps: ASML (monopoly tool maker at ~8% growth) trades ~32x; mature semiconductor at 5-6% growth trades 20-22x; 27x for a dominant foundry at 8% growth with pricing power is squarely in range. Used 27x vs Gordon 29.3x -- a deliberate 8% haircut from the formula, acknowledging uncertainty in the exact LT growth rate. This is conservative but not stacking-conservatively; the bias audit confirms it as one lean among a balanced set. Mature P/E: Gordon gives 17.2x; used 19x (premium justified by moat, recurring revenue, superior returns vs generic industrials). Sources: Theia crux on LT growth and ROIC, Pheme on comps.
lever_4_exit_multiple.sensitivity_range: 25x to 29x exit multiple range moves HPC value by ~$100/ADR. Less decisive than CAGR. ROIC is genuinely second-order since the Gordon formula is not highly ROIC-sensitive at r-g=2.5%.
lever_4_exit_multiple.dominance: TERTIARY -- within the sensitivity range, CAGR moves the value 3-4x more

assumption_list.note: Management guide taken at face value, validated by contracted near-term (N2 sold out, CoWoS booked). Not sandbagged, not inflated.
AI-accel at 55% CAGR from 17-19% of 2025 revenue naturally grows HPC to 70% by 2029. Not heroic; if anything slightly conservative since some sources forecast HPC above 70%.
Q2 2026 guided blended OM 57-58%; HPC-only is higher. 60% for HPC segment at normalization is reasonable and not padded.
Taiwan statutory 20%, effective consistently 16-18% per filings. Not an aggressive assumption.
Used 27x vs the formula's 29.3x. A deliberate haircut. This leans modestly conservative.
Used 33% vs the 2025-derived ~34%. Minor conservatism for overseas dilution risk.
Premium over formula justified by moat. One slight lean high.
Use $70B as a round, acknowledged-approximate number. Slightly below the $72B point estimate from Q1 2026 balance sheet.
Fundamental beta 1.10 correctly captures geopolitical/concentration risk without inflating. Not leaning high.

bias_audit.verdict: NO STACKED BIAS. Three assumptions lean modestly conservative, one leans modestly high. Net bias is not in one direction with enough consistency to constitute a systematic lean. The 3 conservative leans are individually small (exit multiple 8% below Gordon; mature margin 1pp below derived; net cash $2B below point estimate) and partially offset by the mature P/E premium. The base is at the honest center. The CONTRACT §1.3 rule (three or more same-direction = fix it) is borderline triggered on the conservative side, but the magnitudes are small and partially offsetting -- the verdict is CENTER, not conservative-biased, with the understanding that the base value ($430) is essentially AT the market price.

sensitivity_matrix.description: Value per ADR at each combination. Mature segment and net cash held at base values. Dominant lever is CAGR.
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever: CAGR -- a 1pp change moves value ~$8-12; from 16% to 32% moves $232-$629. Exit multiple is secondary: from 22x to 32x at base CAGR = $364 to $496 ($132 range vs $397 CAGR range).
sensitivity_matrix.second_order_lever: ROIC feeds the exit multiple via the Gordon formula but is genuinely second-order: changing ROIC from 25% to 35% at base (g=8%, r=10.5%) moves the Gordon multiple from 26.7x to 31.1x -- only a 4.4x change, within the sensitivity range we already show. ROIC does not move the answer materially given the narrow r-g spread.

street_reconstruction.low_target_analysis: The $354 street low is a multiple-compression thesis (valuation at base-case growth), not a fundamental bear; our $235 bear is a genuine demand-digestion scenario with lower CAGR + lower exit multiple + margin dilution
street_reconstruction.street_thesis_one_line: The street prices TSM as the indispensable structural toll on AI compute; the mean target assumes the 25% guide proves marginally conservative (27-28% actual), with the leading-edge scarcity premium holding through 2027.
street_reconstruction.our_agreement: We agree the leading-edge moat is real, the AI demand pool is large, and the near-term contracted evidence (N2 sold out, CoWoS locked) validates the guide. We also agree TSMC is not overpriced on the base case.
street_reconstruction.our_disagreement: The street's $473 mean target implies ~28% CAGR -- slightly above management's own 25% guide. We take the guide at face value (the honest base). At $432, the stock is essentially at fair value on the base; the BUY is driven by the 12m roll-forward of fundamental value, not by the market being wrong.
street_reconstruction.key_engage: The street's bull consensus is pricing a positive skew to the guide. If the guide proves conservative (AI capex continues to surprise), the stock re-rates toward bull ($583). If the guide proves correct (25%), the stock's 12m return comes purely from earnings compounding, not from multiple expansion -- which is what we model in our $510 target.

fair_value_today.basis: Base SOTP: HPC ExitMultiple $357.91 + Mature P/E $58.62 + net cash $13.50

price_today.note: Market is essentially AT the argued base. Neither cheap nor expensive at the base.

fair_value_12m.basis: #1 ($430.02) rolled forward at blended r=9.7% less dividend $2.85/ADR. Pure intrinsic convergence.

target_12m.basis: FY2026e EPS $15.75 x 32.4x defensible P/E. Documented judgment above pure roll-forward: contracted near-term evidence (N2 sold out through 2Q27, CoWoS locked, FY26 >30% guide being delivered in monthly sales data) supports the market continuing to price AI scarcity premium over next 12 months. Gap from #3 $466 to #4 $510 = $44/ADR of contracted-evidence premium. We do NOT lean to the bull (out-year AI CAGR is EXTRAPOLATED, not contracted).

conviction_argument: MEDIUM because: (1) the base rests on a management guide taken at face value with strong contracted near-term validation (the guide's 25% CAGR is supported by N2/CoWoS bookings and Q1 2026 actuals) -- this would normally push toward HIGH; BUT (2) the out-year AI CAGR (2027-2029) is EXTRAPOLATED on hyperscaler capex durability, which is genuinely unknowable and the source of the wide range ($235 bear to $583 bull, ratio 2.5x); (3) the data quality is CORRECTED (bundle EV and net debt fields were corrupted by TWD/USD mixing); (4) the band ratio 0.81 triggers the wide-band cap to MEDIUM even if other factors were HIGH. The call is BUY on the 12m roll-forward of fundamental value, not on multiple expansion -- the base case returns only 8.5% purely from convergence, but the 12m target of $510 (contracted-evidence lean) delivers 18.7% total return. At MEDIUM conviction, the 12% MoS threshold applies (met: 18.7% > 12%).
t_invariance_note: T-invariance check shows value rising from $263 (T=1.5y) to $467 (T=5.5y). This POSITIVE drift (not T-invariant) is expected for a company where earnings grow faster than the discount rate in the near-to-mid term -- mathematically correct that extending the normalization year increases value when the underlying earnings path grows at 30%+ while r=10.5%. The T=2.5 to T=4.5 range ($309 to $412 for the HPC segment alone) implies the HPC segment is worth $310-$415 depending on the normalization horizon. Adding the mature segment ($59) and net cash ($14), the full range is $382-$485. This spread of $103 (~24% of base) is material but appropriate for a high-growth normalization exercise; it does not indicate the exit multiple and growth path disagree.
